pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json description and setup_task.sh script which references this exact path
Confirmed in task.json description which explicitly states 'save the rendered image to: /home/ga/BlenderProjects/checker_render.png'
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 75 implements verifier check 'if coverage > 0.15:' which grants full points. Metadata claims stricter threshold (0.2) but verifier uses 0.15
Task description states 'Set the Checker Texture Scale to a value between 5 and 30' but verifier.py does not validate scale values - it only extracts and stores the value without range checking
Task description states 'Set the Checker Texture Scale to a value between 5 and 30' but verifier.py does not validate scale values - it only extracts and stores the value without range checking
Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 34 which sets 'suzanne.name = "Suzanne"'. Verifier searches for objects with 'suzan' in name (case-insensitive) at export_result.sh line 95

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic data: a Blender Suzanne monkey head mesh generated programmatically via bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_monkey_add() with subdivision applied. Verified output paths are /home/ga/BlenderProjects/suzanne_checker.blend (blend file) and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/checker_render.png (render output). The target object is named 'Suzanne'. The UV coverage minimum threshold used by the verifier is 0.15, not the 0.2 claimed in metadata. The checker texture scale is supposed to be between 5-30 per the task description, but this is NOT validated by the verifier - it only extracts the scale value without range checking.
